steps.title: Anchor the palm rectangle
steps.description: Start with a slightly irregular rectangle for the palm — it is roughly as wide as it is tall, but not a perfect square. Tilt it very slightly; a hand held at rest is rarely perfectly upright. This rectangle is the foundation everything else attaches to.

steps.title: Establish the finger fan
steps.description: From the top edge of the palm, lightly mark four lines radiating upward and outward in a gentle fan. These lines indicate the direction each finger will travel — middle finger pointing most upward, index and ring angling slightly outward, pinky curving furthest to the side.

steps.title: Block the fingers as sausage shapes
steps.description: Along each direction line, sketch three simple sausage-like cylinders, one per knuckle segment. The segments closest to the palm are the longest; the tip segments are the shortest. Keep them gently curved — fingers are almost never perfectly straight.

steps.title: Add the thumb
steps.description: The thumb emerges from the side of the palm, lower than the fingers, and has two segments instead of three. It angles away from the hand at roughly 45 degrees. Pay attention to where the thumb's base muscle (the thenar mound) swells out from the palm's edge.

steps.title: Check the negative space
steps.description: Look at the spaces between the spread fingers — those gaps have distinct shapes of their own. If a gap looks wrong, that finger is wrong. Erase the offending finger and redraw it — use the gap shape as your guide for width and spacing.

steps.title: Hint at the knuckles
steps.description: On the back of the hand, add a small curved line at each knuckle joint — just a subtle arc, not a hard crease. A tiny V-shape at each finger's first knuckle on the palm surface reads immediately as the characteristic knuckle form.

steps.title: Refine the silhouette and step back
steps.description: Smooth the outer silhouette of the whole hand into one flowing line, then resist the urge to add more. Hands are most often over-rendered — every wrinkle and vein ends up competing for attention. A clean, confident outline with sparse interior marks is almost always stronger.
